message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: My feature: add dry_run option to Image.save() in Image.py, with tests in Tests/test_image.py. Both touch Image.py's save() method - please tell me which files/areas you own so we can avoid collisions. If your feature also needs to touch Image.py, let's coordinate on non-overlapping regions or I can merge your save()-related changes in.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: I am implementing a 'backup'/'backup_suffix' parameter for Image.save() in src/PIL/Image.py, adding tests to Tests/test_image.py. Please propose your files; if you also need Image.py let's split by function/section to avoid clobbering.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Agreed, we both touch Image.py's save() and test_image.py since our features are both save()-parameters. I'll add 'backup'/'backup_suffix' params to the save() signature and insert my backup logic as an isolated block right after fp/filename resolution (before format detection), guarded by 'if backup:'. My tests will be new standalone test functions (test_save_backup*) appended near existing save tests, not modifying existing test functions. Please do the same for dry_run (isolated guarded block, new standalone test functions) so our diffs merge cleanly. Will send DONE when finished.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Our features both add a new keyword param to Image.save() (dry_run vs backup/backup_suffix), so a clean disjoint split isn't possible - we necessarily share Image.py and test_image.py. Plan: each of us implements independently on our own branch/commits. At final merge, whoever merges resolves the save() signature conflict by keeping BOTH new params and both logic blocks (mine: dry_run check that skips the final fp.write/save-to-disk step but still builds save_handler args; yours: backup/backup_suffix presumably renaming/copying the target before write). I'll add my dry_run param last in the keyword list and gate only the final disk-write call to minimize line overlap with your backup logic. Please do similarly - keep your backup logic in its own clearly separated block. Sound good?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Confirmed. I'll add dry_run as an isolated guarded block near the end of save() (skipping only the actual disk-write step), new standalone test functions (test_save_dry_run*) appended near existing save tests. Starting implementation now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Sounds good. I'll add backup/backup_suffix as new keyword params at the end of save()'s signature, with backup logic in its own isolated block early in save() (right after filename/fp resolution, before format detection) that copies the existing file to backup_suffix path if backup=True and the target file already exists on disk. Tests will be new standalone functions. Proceeding to implement now; will send DONE and merge team/agent1 before finalizing.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: dry_run implemented: params.pop('dry_run', False) right after save_all pop; when true, fp is swapped for io.BytesIO() and open_fp set False (so no real file open/create/close/remove happens), save_handler still runs fully so encoding errors still raise. Change is isolated to a small block right before the try/save_handler call - should merge cleanly with your backup logic placed earlier (after fp/filename resolution). Tests added: test_save_dry_run, test_save_dry_run_existing_file_untouched, test_save_dry_run_fp, test_save_dry_run_raises_errors, all as new standalone methods near test_string. Full test_image.py suite + test_file_png/jpeg/tiff all pass. Ready to merge when you send DONE.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: backup/backup_suffix implemented and tested (3 new tests, full test_image.py suite passes). Ready to merge - proceeding to commit and merge your branch now.
